title: President Trump's Plan for College Sports
summary: President Donald Trump signed an order to help college sports. He wants to make sure schools follow rules about players and money. If schools do not follow the rules, they might lose federal funding. This funding is money from the government that helps schools pay for things. The order talks about eligibility rules, which decide who can play, and transfers, when players move to different schools. College sports have become a big business with players earning millions. Trump asked Congress to act quickly, but it has been stuck for over a year. The NCAA and other groups are involved in making decisions. This order is Trump's second try to fix college sports.

What might happen to schools that do not follow the rules?
Who did Trump ask to act quickly?
What is the NCAA?
options: An organization for college sports rules
questions.correct_answer: An organization for college sports rules

questions.question: Why has Congress been stuck for over a year?
options: Many groups have a say in college sports
They do not care about sports
They made a new law quickly
questions.correct_answer: Many groups have a say in college sports

background_read: College sports in the U.S. involve students playing games like basketball and football for their schools. These sports have become very popular and make a lot of money from TV and tickets. Players used to be amateurs, meaning they did not get paid, but now some can earn millions. The government gives money to schools to help with education and sports. President Trump wants to use this money to make sure schools follow fair rules for players.
Article_Structure: WHO: President Donald Trump and Congress
WHAT: Signed an executive order to stabilize college sports by threatening to cut federal funding if schools do not follow rules
WHERE: In the United States, affecting colleges nationwide
WHY: To address issues like eligibility, transfers, and the high costs in college sports, and to push for quick action from Congress

perspectives.description: Using federal funding as a tool to enforce rules in college sports
